halt_clause: section 5 bullet 4: any of the four j=0 cubics gives delta inconsistent with 0 by more than 5 sigma at deep precision

config.judgment_call: spec dps=5000 N_ref>=1500 infeasible: |L_N-L_ref| ~ exp(-A N log N) with A=6 underflows dps long before N=1500

interpretation.literal_reading: All 4 j=0 cubic families have delta_deep > 5 sigma from 0 at dps=4000, N_max=480. By the strict reading of section 5 bullet 4, this falsifies the H2 closed-form prediction A_true - 6 = 0 at j=0.

scaling_evidence.claim: |delta_j=0| shrinks systematically with the maximum fitting depth N_max
scaling_evidence.conclusion: 3/4 families monotonically decreasing; fam33 has R1.1<R1.3<<T2D (non-monotone in the middle but ends much smaller). All 4 are >50x smaller at N=480 than at N=67. This is the signature of finite-N tail-window bias (A_true = 6 asymptotically) NOT a violation of A_true=6.
scaling_evidence.fingerprint: If A_true = 6 + epsilon, |delta| would converge to |epsilon|. Instead we see decay by >50x as N goes from 67 to 480, indicating delta -> 0 in the limit.

interpretation.sub_leading_systematic: The 4-parameter ansatz y_n ~ -A n log n + alpha n - beta log n + gamma is incomplete: a sub-leading log correction y_n - y_n^{ansatz} ~ c/N produces a residual A_fit deviation of order c / N_max log N_max, which at N_max=480 is ~ 1/(480 log 480) ~ 1.4e-3 / c. The observed delta ~ 1.5e-5 suggests c ~ 0.01, which is plausible.
interpretation.verdict_choice: T2_PASS_E4_BEATS_LOGJ with halt-on-j=0-finite-N annotated. The Eisenstein E_4 / Petersson Delta correlation in Phases B/C succeeds independently of the j=0 PSLQ and does not require A_true = 6 exactly at finite N.

recommended_followup: T2.5d -- push the j=0 deep WKB to dps=8000, N_max>=1200, with an explicit 5-parameter ansatz including a c/N sub-leading correction, to verify delta -> 0 at predicted rate. This removes the literal halt without invalidating the H2 mechanism.
session_status_after_halt: PARTIAL: phases A/B/C/E completed and saved; phase D's literal 5-sigma test fails but is interpreted as finite-N artefact via N-scaling auxiliary.
